name: forskolin
summary: Labdane diterpene from Coleus forskohlii root that directly activates adenylyl cyclase to raise intracellular cAMP. Used for fat loss and IOP reduction; modest human evidence for specific applications.
description: Labdane diterpene from Coleus forskohlii root that directly activates adenylyl cyclase to raise intracellular cAMP. Used for fat loss and IOP reduction; modest human evidence for specific applications.
safetyNotes: Mostly preclinical or source-extract evidence. Isolated-compound safety is not equivalent to food/herb exposure; use caution with pregnancy, liver/kidney disease, and polypharmacy.

claimMap.claim: v10.3: Forskolin has limited small-trial data and inconsistent weight outcomes; block fat-loss monetization.
claimMap.notes: v10.3: Forskolin has limited small-trial data and inconsistent weight outcomes; block fat-loss monetization.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Human studies used Coleus forskohlii extract protocols such as 250 mg of 10% forskolin twice daily, but evidence is limited

safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: PubMed PMID 16129715. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 16129715. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

dosage: standardized extract or essential-oil-free preparation when oral use is intended
typical_dosage: standardized extract or essential-oil-free preparation when oral use is intended
contraindications: antihypertensive medications such as calcium channel blockers or ACE inhibitors or alpha-blockers due to additive blood-pressure-lowering effect and risk of symptomatic hypotension
anticoagulant or antiplatelet medications such as warfarin due to antiplatelet activity and increased bleeding risk
polycystic kidney disease due to cAMP-driven cyst growth or severe kidney disease or severe liver disease due to altered drug metabolism and elimination
pregnancy or breastfeeding due to uterine-stimulant effects and insufficient safety data
